text.title: Web Scraping Pagination Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every paginated walk must satisfy plus run-the-checklist + skip-this-methodology. The walk-report validator enforces them.

rule.statement: Pagination pattern MUST be classified BEFORE writing the walker: next-button | infinite-scroll | load-more. Mismatched walker against pattern causes infinite loops or premature stop.
rule.rationale: Each pattern has a distinct stop condition; mismatching the walker is the principal cause of paginated-scrape failures.

rule.statement: Stop condition matches pattern: next-button → button absent OR disabled-attribute set; infinite-scroll → `document.body.scrollHeight` did not grow after N seconds idle; load-more → button absent OR scrolled-out-of-viewport. Each must be tested against a real page sample BEFORE the production walk.
rule.rationale: Using "next button exists" when the button is disabled but still in DOM produces an infinite loop within seconds.

rule.statement: Every collected item passes through dedup by stable key (item id, URL, or sha1 of normalized fields). Duplicate count is recorded in the walk report. Without dedupe, infinite-scroll overlap silently inflates row count.
rule.rationale: Infinite-scroll fetches overlap by design; dedupe is mandatory, not optional.

rule.statement: Every page open MUST be wrapped in try/finally that closes the page. Concurrent walks use `p-limit` (TS) / `asyncio.Semaphore` (Py) with a documented cap; default cap is 3 unless infra allows more.
rule.rationale: Leaked pages consume 50–200 MB each; a 100-URL batch without management OOMs any standard VPS.

rule.statement: Walk report records pool_high_water_mark = max simultaneous pages observed. Must be ≤ documented cap. Drift here signals a leak in the finally block.
rule.rationale: Without the high-water-mark metric, leaks go undetected until the OOM kills the run.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (pool, item key, stop selector), execute: detect-pattern → cap-pool → walk → dedupe → emit walk-report. Emit pool_high_water_mark even on small runs.
rule.rationale: Skipping the high-water metric defers leak detection to the OOM event.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) the source exposes an offset/limit API — hit it, (b) single-page source, (c) one-off scrape <100 items — manual is cheaper, (d) source aggressively rate-limits — load `web-scraping-resilience` first.
rule.rationale: Pool overhead is wasted when an API endpoint exists or the run is too small to OOM.
